window_fidelity.evidence: clean_iso: glazed windows hosted in wall faces on the two center rooms, the right suite (sliding), plus small awning windows and the terrace glass railing — consistent with windows3d=4. Top view shows matching sill marks; no orphan glass panels.

scale_rotation.evidence: clean_side_by_side: SKP top aligns to PDF orientation and proportions — kitchen top-left, living center, terraço bottom-left, suites right, bath core center; no flip, rotation, or aspect mismatch.

door_fidelity.evidence: clean_top shows ~6 door leaves with swing arcs (kitchen top, cyan closet, central bath junction, right suite→far-right room), each hinged on a wall/jamb. The central '+' pillar cluster is busy but every leaf is hosted; clean_iso confirms brown leaves standing inside real openings, none floating.

room_fidelity.evidence: clean_top: each room carries a distinct closed floor fill (kitchen, living/sala, terraço, two baths, right suites) with no color bleeding past walls; count and placement mirror the PDF rooms.

global_visual.evidence: Overall SKP top/iso is a faithful, enclosed reproduction of the PDF layout; no leaks, no missing walls, no misplaced fixtures visible at render resolution.

wall_fidelity.evidence: clean_top: exterior perimeter traces continuously with no erased segments; interior partitions and the stepped terrace/suite jogs match the PDF footprint. clean_iso: all rooms read as fully bounded solids, no open-ended stubs.

window_fidelity.evidence: Four glazed openings visible: bottom of lavender bathroom, bottom of far-right small suite, the living/terrace sliding glass at bottom-center, plus the terrace glass band lower-left; in clean_iso these appear as punched, sill-mounted windows plus a 4-panel terrace railing. Count and placement consistent with windows3d=4; no full-height void or misplaced pane seen.

scale_rotation.evidence: SKP top orientation, aspect ratio and room arrangement align with the PDF panel in the side-by-side (kitchen top-left, terrace bottom-left, suites right); no mirroring or rotation offset detected.

door_fidelity.evidence: Doors render as brown swing leaves in expected openings (top kitchen/living doorway, LAVABO, terrace side, right suite top, bathroom). Swing directions and positions are consistent with the PDF; no leaf floating detached from a wall host.

room_fidelity.evidence: Each room carries a distinct floor fill (cream kitchen, cyan living, green terrace, pink suite, lavender bath, yellow suites) with no color bleeding across walls; room count and adjacency match the PDF panel in corrupted_side_by_side.

global_visual.evidence: Overall the top view is a faithful reproduction of the PDF floor plan; clean_iso shows coherent extruded walls with floors and glazing and no floating/orphan geometry.

wall_fidelity.evidence: Perimeter reads as fully enclosed on all four sides and interior partitions match the PDF layout, BUT the central junction where the LAVABO/SUITE/BANHEIRO cluster meets the living and right-suite (approx center of corrupted_top, between the cyan, pink, lavender and yellow rooms) shows a small isolated wall block with door leaves converging on it and short gaps around it. The wall line through that node reads as fragmented rather than a single continuous partition. It plausibly corresponds to a legitimate doorframe pier in the PDF, so this is flagged for deterministic opening_host_audit re-check rather than called a hard break.

findings.location: central room junction between LAVABO (cyan), SUITE (pink), BANHEIRO (lavender) and right SUITE (yellow), roughly center of frame
findings.evidence: A small isolated wall block sits at the four-room junction with door leaves on either side and short gaps separating it from the adjoining partitions, so the central wall line reads as fragmented rather than continuous. May be a legitimate doorframe pier matching the PDF; recommend opening_host_audit to confirm the wall host is intact and not a true stub/gap. No non-enclosed perimeter or erased exterior segment was observed elsewhere.
